channel.title: Living Savior Lutheran Church
channel.description: Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.

item.title: President's Message
item.description:
Newsletter Input 31 August 2020

The mission of Living Savior is to know the love of Christ and serve God so the Holy Spirit can work through us so we can bring others to Christ. While we need to take safety precautions during the pandemic, our mission continues. We strengthen our faith by attending worship (in-person or on-line), regularly studying the Word, supporting each other, and reaching out to the community.

I want to thank the 2019-2020 Board of Servant Leaders (BOSL), led by Chuck Froemke, for their dedication and tireless work. The BOSL tackled COVID-19 by developing and implementing meticulous health precautions, insuring we have weekly Services while protecting parishioners. Of particular note are the on-going efforts of the Worship team: Pastor Lissy, Vicar Brad (now Vicar Tim), Dr. Horton, Dr. Benson, Candie Jefferson, Sid Heetland, Mary Heetland, Dave Huxsoll, Lori Hults, and Cameron Gallarno. Their dedication and unwavering commitment is a shining example of living one’s faith.

Volunteer opportunities abound. Social Ministry, Outreach, the Pre-School, and Communication (i.e., live streaming of our weekly worship service) are the most visible to the public, but other ministries such as Education, Property, Youth, and Stewardship need volunteers as well. I encourage everyone to actively support at least one ministry throughout the year.

Blessings,
Steve Galing

item.title: Pastor Andy’s Message
item.description:
When I was growing up, I lived for baseball. I loved it. When I entered into sixth grade, I had to get glasses!! But I remember one game right after I got glasses. I was really having problems adjusting to them. I missed two fly balls and got our team behind several runs. I came to the bunch with my head bowed. I felt so bad that I didn’t want to look at anyone. Suddenly, I felt a hand on my shoulder and there was my coach (who thankfully was my father!), and he said: “Hold your head up!” When I looked up, I saw a coach who still believed in me and friends there to encourage me. I wanted to look down, for that’s how I felt, but they would not let me.

And that’s what I find that stands out to me about the hymn “My  Faith Looks Up to Thee.” What do you do when you find yourself  in way over your head? When you have really blown it? Where do  you turn? To whom do you look?

My Faith Looks Up to Thee, Thou Lamb of Calvary, 
Savior divine. Now hear me as I pray; Take all my guilt away; 
o let me from this day Be wholly Thine.
- [Lutheran Service Book #702]

I have found in my life that there are lots of experiences that will make us want to fasten our eyes to the ground, that will sink us into depression, so that we withdraw into our own little world. All we then will see is our pain, our guilt, our limitations, what we can’t do, what we don’t have.

Let me tell you, looking down all the time makes you down! It limits you. You can see only a few feet. That’s the circumference of your world! But it doesn’t have to be! Our eyes are to be up, not down! Our vision is set to the horizon! For ours is a faith in a God bigger than our circumstances, a God who is “enthroned in the heavens.” When life tells you, “Look down! Get down on yourself!” that’s when you got to remember to hold your head up! To force your eyes to the heavens, to remind yourself of the One who made you and who is ever with you.  

 God’s Peace This month! 
Pastor Andrew C. Lissy

item.title: Lslc September 2020 Newsletter
item.description:
October Happenings

Living Savior Preschool opened October 1st for the new school year. We’re so excited to have the children with us again! Due to COVID restrictions we aren’t able to have any in house programs or field trips at this time so things are a bit different than they’ve been in the past. Still, our year is off to a great start and the children all seem to love being back in school!

Chapel Lessons

Each Wednesday morning throughout the school year, the children at Living Savior gather in the Sanctuary for a Chapel lesson with either Pastor Andy or Vicar Tim. This is an important part of our annual curriculum at Living Savior Preschool as we teach the word of God through song and prayer and try to relate the message of the bible story so that it has meaning in the children’s young lives. We look forward to another wonderful year of Chapel lessons as we continue to teach the children that Jesus touches every part of our day!

Preschool Openings

We have a couple of openings so if you or someone you know is looking for a quality Christian preschool, please call the preschool office at 703-352-4208.

Thank you!

We would like to thank all those who have helped us prepare for the new school year, especially Jonathan and Sarah Loesch, Marc Morgan, Steve Galing, Miki Kolton and Cathi Willuweit. Last but certainly not least, many thanks to Vicar Tim, who was knee deep in mud helping to install our new circle cycle!

item.title: The Congregational Choir
item.description:
LIVING SAVIOR WORSHIP & MUSIC

Have you ever taken the opportunity to look through Scripture and see how many times it talks about “Singing?” The Psalms are loaded with references to singing. In fact, there are around ninety instances when the psalmists directs the reader to “sing.” Psalm 33 uses the word three times in the first three verses. “Sing for joy in the Lord . . .” “Sing to Him . . .” “Sing to Him a new song.” Singing is an act of worship and praise that has been a part of the lives of God’s people throughout history. (“Then Moses and the Sons of Israel sang this song to the Lord . . .” Ex. 15:1)

The Church throughout her history has had choirs and special ensembles to ensure that there would be singing in its worship. Over the years, in some situations, the choirs have been thought of as “the singers” to the neglect of the congregation. The truth, however, is that the congregation is the choir. Congregational song and praise have been and continue to be at the heart of our corporate worship.

One of my dear friends and mentors, the late Dr. Howie Stevenson, was Minister of Music at The First Evangelical Free Church in Fullerton, CA where Chuck Swindoll served for many years as pastor. Howie always talked about the importance of the “Congregational Choir.” He was always encouraging the Body of Christ to sing with enthusiasm and fervor. In fact, he even coined a little phrase that I borrowed and used in my ministry for many years. “ALWAYS KEEP ON SINGING!” Howie was right! The congregational choir is the most important choir and they need to keep on singing, especially in these days when our ensembles are unable to meet to practice and rehearse.

It is vitally important that as we gather as the Body of Christ, we “Always Keep on Singing!” It is through our collective voices and praise that we can echo the Song of the Church, “Christ is Risen!” May our songs of praise always bring honor to the name of Jesus.

Dr. Wallace Horton,
Minister of Worship & Music

CANCELLATIONS

Due to the fact that we are still being guided by directives and regulations surrounding the Covid 19 virus, our various worship ministries – Chancel Choir, Modern Worship Ensemble, Lay Readers, Acolytes - are not able to meet at this time. All of us are praying for the time when we can resume our various activities and ministries.

BIBLE STUDY – “Biblical Characters Who Faced the Same Challenges We Do”

You are invited to participate in a Zoom Bible Study based on Six Biblical Characters Who Faced the Same Challenges We Do. The study begins on Sunday, September 6 at 6:30 and will last for six weeks. It will be led by Drs. Horton and Benson. If you would like to participate, email Dr. Meghan Benson at (mabenson2 @gmail.com) for a Zoom link invitation.
